content.full_text:
/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 11, 1988


Marjorie Offord
4146 Van Buren Boulevard
Riverside, CA 92503

Re: 88-019

Dear Ms. Offord:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 4, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[handwritten signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

JP:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

A-88-019

JAN 4 9 3 P P 87

December 30, 1987


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA    95814

Attention: Kevin Braaten-Moen

Dear Sir:

This is to confirm our phone conversation of December 29 with regard
to Campaign Disclosure Form 490 for a Riverside County Supervisor.

It is my understanding from our discussion that if a contribution is
received in the mail from a Company or Corporation the name of that
Company or Corporation is listed as the Contributor no matter who
attends the Fund Raiser or who signs the check.

Also if several businesses with different names are under the control
of the same person or persons, the total amount contributed by all
must be accumulated in one figure for the year.

Thank you for your assistance and I do hope  that I have interpreted
your answers correctly.

Yours very truly,

Marjorie Offord
4146 Van Buren Boulevard
Riverside, California  92503

Phone: (714)689-1492

Yours very truly,

Marjorie Offord
4146 Van Buren Boulevard

sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks clarification on how to properly report contributions on Campaign Disclosure Form 490 for a Riverside County Supervisor, specifically regarding corporate contributions and aggregation of contributions from multiple businesses under common control.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and confirms that contributions received from companies or corporations should be listed under the company/corporation name regardless of individual involvement, and that contributions from multiple businesses under the same control must be aggregated into a single annual figure.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be an acknowledgment/receipt letter rather than a formal advice letter. No dedicated QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The substantive legal guidance appears in the requester's letter (dated December 30, 1987) confirming phone advice from FPPC staff, while the January 11, 1988 letter is purely procedural. Extracted question and conclusion from the requester's confirmation of phone conversation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester seeks clarification on how to properly report contributions on Campaign Disclosure Form 490 for a Riverside County Supervisor, specifically regarding corporate contributions and aggregation of contributions from multiple businesses under common control.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and confirms that contributions received from companies or corporations should be listed under the company/corporation name regardless of individual involvement, and that contributions from multiple businesses under the same control must be aggregated into a single annual figure.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC acknowledgment letter confirming receipt of an informal advice request from Marjorie Offord regarding proper reporting of corporate contributions on Form 490 for a Riverside County Supervisor election. The letter references a prior phone conversation with Kevin Braaten-Moen and outlines the FPPC's response timeline.
